title: Cursed Island
subtitle: A season of fractured alliances, cursed advantages, comeback ghosts, and one underdog who refused to leave before the volcano answered.

winner.name: Miriam
winner.keyStats: Miriam wins Cursed Island with 1 challenge win, 1 immunity, 8 votes against, and survival through repeated minority positions, idol scares, revotes, tie pressure, and an endgame where nearly every remaining player saw her as dangerous too late.
winner.strategy: Miriam plays a patient pressure game: she lets louder players become shields, builds deep trust with Thom and later Eureka, keeps her Extra Vote long enough to shape early momentum, and repeatedly survives by redirecting attention onto players who appear more volatile. Her best work comes late, when she turns the Thom bond from liability into leverage and beats him at the summit with an emotional mind game instead of raw speed.
winner.legacy: Miriam leaves as the season's definitive survivor: underestimated at the dock, painted as a target at merge, nearly cracked by paranoia, and still standing with the million when the volcano erupts. Her win is remembered less as a clean march and more as a stubborn, bruising refusal to be erased.

placements.name: Miriam
placements.notes: Miriam wins by turning survival into strategy and using her bond with Thom as both shield and final weapon.
placements.story: Miriam arrives with the posture of someone who has already survived worse than Cursed Island. Early on, she is abrasive, guarded, and constantly one bad vote away from disaster, but every time the numbers move against her she finds another route through. Her game sharpens after the merge, where she stops chasing control openly and starts letting other players expose themselves first. The Outwit Club gives her structure, Thom gives her protection, and her own reads give her timing. By the finale, she is exhausted but still calculating, and when Thom reaches the summit ahead of her she does not beat him with strength. She beats him with history, memory, and one perfect plea before throwing the dummy first.
keyMoments: Ep.2: Finds an Extra Vote while collecting water and hides it from everyone.
Ep.4: Forms The Camp Takeover with Gabby after realizing both are socially exposed.
Ep.9: Plays her Extra Vote to bury Sterling and survive another fractured Teal vote.
Ep.12: Wins Way of the Warrior immunity by stealing the bonsai from Sasquatchanakwa.
Ep.13: Helps drive the Ellie vote after reading the jury danger correctly.
Ep.17: Survives a 2-2 tie when Fiore flips on the revote to eliminate Alec.
Ep.18: Wins Final Immunity and then defeats Thom at the volcano through a final emotional mind game.

placements.name: Thom
placements.notes: Thom reaches the final two as the season's emotional anchor and physical workhorse, only to lose when compassion slows his hand at the summit.
placements.story: Thom starts as the teammate everyone wants, and that becomes both his gift and his curse. He rescues people, protects people, feeds people, and quietly becomes the center of too many hearts at once. His bonds with Jake, Dan, Sterling, Miriam, and Eureka carry him deep, but they also make his decisions heavier than everyone else's. As the merge turns colder, Thom learns to cut names he cares about, but never completely shuts the door on feeling. He wins when he has to, survives repeated target spikes, and takes out Fiore in the final joust. At the volcano, he is ahead, but Miriam reaches the one place his game is vulnerable: the part of him that still cares.
keyMoments: Ep.2: Saves Gabby from drowning during Rock the Dock, cementing his hero image.
Ep.5: Exposes Seraphine's backstage sabotage after his Talent Show disaster.
Ep.7: Helps flip the vote onto Grett despite The Firewall collapsing around him.
Ep.13: Wins Monster Cash immunity by outlasting Eureka in the final showdown.
Ep.16: Wins invincibility while handcuffed to rival Alec in the Tri-Armed Triathlon.
Ep.17: Wins immunity in Planes, Trains & Hot Air Mobiles and protects Miriam at the vote.
Ep.18: Beats Fiore in the joust, then loses the million after hesitating against Miriam at the crater.

placements.name: Fiore
placements.notes: Fiore is the season's central villain, surviving on manipulation and one huge advantage before losing the final joust inches from the final two.
placements.story: Fiore enters promising to enjoy watching people leave, and she delivers. She steals credit, plants doubts, forges notes, weaponizes grudges, and somehow keeps crawling back from spots where the numbers should finish her. Her rivalry with Ellie burns across the season, while her shifting partnerships with Alec, Gabby, Seraphine, and Miriam are always temporary and always useful. The Second Life Amulet is her masterpiece of survival, turning a majority against her into Eureka's elimination and taking Gabby with it. But Fiore's game has a cost: by the finale, the jury and players alike know exactly what she is. She fights Thom in the joust, asks the crowd for support, gets silence, and falls smiling like a villain who knows she made the season.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Drives the Ashley vote while secretly voting Ellie, fracturing The Barricade immediately.
Ep.3: Helps vote out Will after a simmering feud becomes the tribe's decision.
Ep.8: Survives a tied vote when Nick loses the tiebreaker.
Ep.11: Wins Lucky Hunt immunity after grabbing the right chest.
Ep.12: Finds the Second Life Amulet on Exile Island and tells nobody.
Ep.16: Activates the Second Life Amulet, defeats Eureka in a Speed Puzzle, and survives the vote.
Ep.18: Loses the final joust to Thom 2-1 and finishes third.

placements.name: Alec
placements.notes: Alec plays one of the sharpest strategic games on the island but becomes too visible as the endgame narrows.
placements.story: Alec enters already mapping alliance structures before most players know where camp is. He builds gadgets, reads conversations he was never part of, and keeps finding ways to make the vote pass through him without looking obvious enough to stop. But every clean move leaves fingerprints, and by the time Grett publicly calls him the strategic center, the target never fully leaves his back. His rivalry with Thom hardens into open hostility, and his relationship with Miriam becomes a chess match played with smiles that never reach the eyes. He survives several spots where he should go home, including a fire-making tiebreaker against Dan. At final four, though, the board is too small, Thom holds firm, and Fiore cuts her losses.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Builds the Mirror Reflector and gives Purple a major tactical edge in Ice Ice Baby.
Ep.3: Votes Will out despite their bond, proving he will cut allies for position.
Ep.6: Is exposed by Grett as the player calling votes behind the scenes.
Ep.11: Breaks from The Outwit Club by voting Gabby while publicly committing to Ellie.
Ep.12: Beats Dan in a fire-making tiebreaker to stay alive.
Ep.13: Uses the Miriam cover plan to quietly help eliminate Ellie.
Ep.17: Loses the revote to Miriam after Fiore flips to avoid rocks.

placements.name: Gabby
placements.notes: Gabby stockpiles advantages and survives through chaos, but the Tied Destinies twist removes her as collateral beside Eureka.
placements.story: Gabby plays sideways from the start, finding a Vote Block, later a Vote Steal, and rarely moving in the same direction as the room. She is emotional, observant, and dangerous when underestimated, but her relationships constantly swing between warmth and rupture. Her rivalry with Miriam becomes one of the season's nastiest personal threads, while her bonds with Jake and Eureka give her late-game life. She makes the move that sends Dan out, proving she can cut a supposed number when the relationship breaks. But the endgame twist is cruel: she is not the target, not the central threat, not even the duel loser. She is chained to Eureka's fate and leaves because Fiore beats someone else.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Finds the Vote Block near the Teal shelter.
Ep.2: Is rescued from drowning by Thom during Rock the Dock.
Ep.5: Finds the Vote Steal and hides it from the tribe.
Ep.7: Sets the pace for Purple in Up the Creek and earns MVP paddler status.
Ep.12: Steals Alec's vote and redirects it to Dan, helping force Dan into the tiebreaker.
Ep.13: Votes Alec while trying to protect Eureka from the Jake Core collapse.
Ep.16: Is eliminated as collateral when Eureka loses Fiore's Second Life duel.

placements.name: Eureka
placements.notes: Eureka is eliminated twice: first by pure chance, then by Fiore's Second Life Amulet after becoming too trusted and too visible.
placements.story: Eureka begins as warmth, energy, and glue, the kind of player people underestimate because they enjoy being around her. Her first exit is one of the season's cruelest: a rock draw sends her out without a single original vote against her. But Cursed Island refuses to let ghosts stay buried, and Eureka fights through Aftermayhem to return with sharper instincts. Back in the game, she becomes a crucial Outwit Club number and a social force nobody can comfortably attack head-on. Her bond with Thom and Miriam makes her powerful, but it also makes her obvious. At final six, she aims at Fiore and gets hit by the one advantage that can rewrite the ending.
keyMoments: Ep.5: Is eliminated by rock draw despite receiving no votes.
Ep.12: Wins the Aftermayhem board race and returns to the game.
Ep.13: Rejoins the Outwit Club and helps eliminate Ellie.
Ep.14: Wins the Greece's Pieces reward and chooses Thom, straining other bonds.
Ep.16: Targets Fiore with The Collective at the Tied Destinies vote.
Ep.16: Loses the Speed Puzzle duel after Fiore activates the Second Life Amulet.
Ep.16: Her elimination also takes Gabby out as collateral.

placements.name: Jake
placements.notes: Jake's emotional game creates deep bonds and deep resentment, ending when Chef cuts him for cowardice in the woods.
placements.story: Jake is liked immediately, and for a while that is enough to move through the game. His bond with Thom becomes the season's defining showmance, a source of protection, suspicion, and eventually heartbreak. Jake tries to build structures around himself, from The Firewall to temporary merge coalitions, but he often cannot fully commit when votes hit people he cares about. His name becomes synonymous with overplaying, whispered side deals, and emotional reactions that other players learn to exploit. By the end, he is isolated from old allies and too tangled in personal feuds with Miriam, Ellie, and Fiore. His exit is absurd and painful: Chef, not the tribe, ends his story after a survival challenge full of fear, loyalty, and humiliation.
keyMoments: Ep.2: Forms The Firewall with Sterling after a shared early bond.
Ep.5: Refuses to vote Wix and helps trigger the chaos that sends Eureka out by rocks.
Ep.7: Flips onto Grett after her overplaying becomes impossible to ignore.
Ep.10: Overplays during the open merge vote and receives flipped votes from Fiore and Miriam.
Ep.11: Breaks The Outwit Club by voting Miriam instead of Ellie.
Ep.12: Is betrayed by Thom in the mountain climb when Thom shoves his water away.
Ep.14: Is eliminated by Chef after the Are We There Yeti survival ordeal.

placements.name: Ellie
placements.notes: Ellie plays aggressively with an idol, survives a massive vote with a Super Idol, and finally falls when the island unites against her strategic threat level.
placements.story: Ellie finds the season's first idol and immediately becomes a player everyone talks around, not through. Her game is sharp, paranoid, and relentless; she plants notes, steals keys, pushes whisper campaigns, and keeps trying to collapse Fiore's position. Her Super Idol play is one of the season's defining advantage moments, turning a pile of votes into someone else's funeral. But her constant motion exhausts the camp, and the jury math around her becomes impossible to ignore. She keeps surviving by narrow margins until Eureka, Miriam, Thom, and even Alec decide the same thing at once. When she leaves, she does not leave quietly; she names the power structure and makes sure the jury knows who took her out.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Finds the Purple Hidden Immunity Idol before anyone can clock her game.
Ep.5: Gets caught crafting a fake idol by Dan, damaging her credibility.
Ep.8: Breaks from The Bloc plan and helps send Nick into the tiebreaker.
Ep.10: Wins the Idol Wager and upgrades her idol into a Super Idol.
Ep.11: Plays the Super Idol after votes are read, canceling four votes against her.
Ep.11: Steals Gabby's key during Lucky Hunt in front of the entire camp.
Ep.13: Is voted out 4-2-1-1 after the Outwit Club identifies her as the strategic center.

placements.name: Dan
placements.notes: Dan controls important early votes and plays like a technician, but his social misread of Gabby sends him into a fatal tiebreaker.
placements.story: Dan arrives calling the game chess, and for a long stretch he plays like he believes it. He helps engineer Drew's exit, becomes Thom's quiet strategic partner, and repeatedly positions himself as the person with the best read in the room. But the same confidence that makes him dangerous also blinds him to emotional fallout. His relationships with Gabby and Miriam fray as he treats votes like math problems that should solve cleanly. At merge, he keeps trying to hold shifting coalitions together, but the island has become too personal for pure calculation. In the fire-making tiebreaker against Alec, Dan's game finally runs out of oxygen.
keyMoments: Ep.2: Helps drive Drew's vote-out and is named by Drew as the one pulling strings.
Ep.3: Designs Teal's Human Stack plan and gets them first to cut the totem.
Ep.4: Builds deep trust with Thom during the jungle night and dawn conversations.
Ep.10: Joins the unstable Three Headed Snake at merge.
Ep.11: Finds Lucky Hunt game intel at the auction but cannot convert it into safety.
Ep.12: Votes Gabby against the Outwit Club plan, triggering the feud that burns him.
Ep.12: Loses the fire-making tiebreaker to Alec and exits admitting he misread Gabby.

placements.name: Lill
placements.notes: Lill's loyalty carries her deep, but her honest game becomes expendable once idols and split votes take over.
placements.story: Lill begins as the reliable one, the player whose word feels heavier than most people's promises. Her bonds with Seraphine and Ellie become defining pillars, and for a while they give her cover in a volatile Purple tribe. She sees more than people expect, reads body language well, and often steadies allies who are spiraling. But Lill's loyalty also makes her predictable, and predictability is deadly when the merge turns into idol insurance and split-vote math. After Seraphine leaves, she loses the emotional shield that kept her grounded. When Ellie's Super Idol detonates the plan, Lill becomes the cleanest casualty.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Reads that Fiore's name is floating without being told directly.
Ep.2: Bonds with Ellie on the dock after Rock the Dock.
Ep.4: Forms The Circle with Seraphine and Nick before Fiore drifts away.
Ep.8: Stands by Seraphine and Ellie as The Bloc begins cracking.
Ep.10: Protects Seraphine by voting Gabby instead of following anti-Seraphine pressure.
Ep.11: Is voted out after Ellie's Super Idol cancels the main plan.
Ep.11: Leaves saying loyalty gets you a jury seat, not Final Tribal.

placements.name: Seraphine
placements.notes: Seraphine schemes, bonds hard with Lill, and loses at merge after a public tie exposes every fragile faction.
placements.story: Seraphine opens as a smiling schemer who wants to be three steps ahead, but her best game is not strategy; it is loyalty with Lill. She hides in plain sight, gathers information, and lets louder players make themselves targets. Her pre-merge run includes sabotage, social reads, and alliances that work until they become too visible. At the merge, she is pulled between The Three Headed Snake, The Circle, and survival instinct, and the open vote gives her nowhere to hide. She ties with Ellie and loses the strength tiebreaker, a brutal end for a player who had avoided direct confrontation for so long. Her final words are not about strategy, but about leaving Lill alone.
keyMoments: Ep.2: Captains Purple to a Rock the Dock win despite questionable assignments.
Ep.4: Forms an unbreakable bond with Lill and helps create The Circle.
Ep.5: Sabotages Thom's Talent Show props, causing his stage disaster.
Ep.7: Helps form The Bloc with Fiore, Lill, and Nick.
Ep.10: Votes Gabby in the open vote while caught between competing plans.
Ep.10: Loses a head-to-head strength tiebreaker to Ellie and becomes the first merge juror.
Ep.10: Exits worried about Lill surviving without her.

placements.name: Sterling
placements.notes: Sterling flashes as a physical force and emotional connector before personal tension with Jake and Miriam turns him into the final pre-merge boot.
placements.story: Sterling enters with the confidence of a challenge beast, and early on he looks like the kind of player tribes keep because they need muscle. He bonds with Jake, Eureka, Wix, Thom, and Dan, but those connections never settle into a stable alliance. The Firewall gives him structure, then collapses under miscommunication, betrayal, and quiet resentment. His challenge résumé grows just as his social safety shrinks. By the time Teal hits one last pre-merge tribal, Sterling is no longer just useful; he is threatening, disliked by Jake, and distrusted by Miriam. Miriam's Extra Vote turns a bad night into a decisive one.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Is voted out by first impressions but saved by the swap twist onto Teal.
Ep.2: Finishes as Teal's fastest swimmer in Rock the Dock.
Ep.4: Powers through the Colossus and bonds with Wix and Thom.
Ep.5: Misvotes Jake instead of Wix, damaging The Firewall beyond repair.
Ep.8: Wins a Bone Battle round against Seraphine for Teal.
Ep.9: Finds a Teal brand in the bungee sheep hunt.
Ep.9: Is eliminated after Miriam uses an Extra Vote to seal the numbers.

placements.name: Nick
placements.notes: Nick floats, flips, and occasionally shocks the tribe with competence before losing a tiebreaker after The Bloc fractures.
placements.story: Nick starts as the relaxed, slightly unfiltered player nobody quite knows how to handle. He finds moments of real usefulness, including a perfect fish-kiss leg and strong early challenge flashes, but his strategic loyalty is never trusted. He moves around The Circle and The Bloc without ever seeming fully inside either one. His vote lies and side deals keep him alive briefly, but every alliance eventually labels him a flipper. In his final vote, the tribe cannot agree whether Fiore or Nick should go, and the tiebreaker becomes the season's way of punishing drift. Even leaving, Nick makes noise by exposing The Bloc and Gabby's advantage history.
keyMoments: Ep.2: Crushes the fish-kiss leg and declares people underestimated him.
Ep.3: Gets shoved off the totem by Seraphine, a move Alec catches.
Ep.5: Quietly detaches from The Circle and goes solo.
Ep.6: Is targeted by Alec despite the public Grett plan.
Ep.8: Votes Alec instead of Fiore, breaking The Bloc.
Ep.8: Loses the challenge tiebreaker after tying with Fiore.
Ep.8: Exposes The Bloc and Gabby's advantage history on the way out.

placements.name: Grett
placements.notes: Grett brings sabotage, grudges, and chaos, but overplaying makes her impossible to keep.
placements.story: Grett is targeted before camp even begins, survives through the swap twist, and immediately turns resentment into fuel. Her biggest early move is invisible: sabotaging Purple's fort and framing Ashley, a dirty play that sets the season's tone. She talks like she controls the room even when the room is already turning on her, and her reads are often strong enough to scare people. The elimination swap gives her one more life, but it also leaves her surrounded by people she has burned. On Teal, she tries to scramble through too many doors at once. At tribal, the whispering becomes the reason everyone needs.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Is first-impression voted out but swapped to Purple instead of eliminated.
Ep.1: Sabotages Purple's fort and frames Ashley for the collapse.
Ep.3: Helps target Fiore from inside a fractured anti-Seraphine bloc.
Ep.5: Is voted out but saved by the elimination swap, choosing Gabby in exchange.
Ep.6: Blows up tribal by exposing Alec's control, Ellie's idol, and The Circle.
Ep.7: Overplays with too many side deals and becomes the consensus boot.
Ep.7: Leaves with a furious crashout speech about everyone playing safe.

placements.name: Wix
placements.notes: Wix creates noise, finds one heroic flag moment, and then gets cut by Fiore's emissary power after Teal turns on them.
placements.story: Wix arrives promising to change the mood, and for a while they do exactly that. They are messy, funny, occasionally useful, and frequently in the wrong place at the wrong time. Their high point is enormous: finding the real flag in Ice Ice Baby and delivering Teal's first immunity win. But Rock the Dock damages their credibility badly when they get lost at sea and then captain poorly. Their rivalry with Miriam grows, their camp schemes wear thin, and the tribe starts treating them as both social threat and challenge liability. Even after surviving the vote chaos, Fiore's emissary pick finishes the job.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Captures Purple's real flag and wins Ice Ice Baby for Teal.
Ep.2: Gets lost at sea during Rock the Dock and misses the relay.
Ep.2: Serves as a bad captain with questionable assignments that help sink Teal.
Ep.3: Clashes with Miriam during the totem race, deepening their rivalry.
Ep.4: Is sent to Exile Island and finds nothing.
Ep.5: Lands in a tie with Miriam after a fractured Teal vote.
Ep.5: Is removed by Fiore's emissary pick after surviving the vote itself.

placements.name: Will
placements.notes: Will plays loyal, useful, and perceptive, but Alec's pivot cuts him before his social game can mature.
placements.story: Will begins as the steady worker, the one who calls practical challenge plans and notices when other people are lying. He sees through Fiore before most do, and he reads Grett's wrong answers with unnerving calm. His problem is that being useful makes him visible and being likeable makes him dangerous. He builds enough trust with Alec to believe the relationship might carry weight. Instead, Alec cuts him as a jury threat while Fiore drives the personal tension into a vote. Will leaves feeling betrayed less by the game than by the person he thought was real with him.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Finds calm in camp by sitting with a turtle while Purple fractures around him.
Ep.1: Joins The Barricade's Ashley vote before the alliance immediately cracks.
Ep.2: Crushes the chug leg in Rock the Dock for Purple.
Ep.2: Notices Fiore stealing credit and quietly boils over it.
Ep.3: Pushes Saw & Climb as Purple's totem method and gets the tribe moving.
Ep.3: Is voted out by Seraphine, Alec, Fiore, and Lill after Alec pivots.
Ep.3: Calls out Alec in his final words for making their bond feel fake.

placements.name: Drew
placements.notes: Drew works hard and performs well once, but gets labeled a liability early and never builds the protection to survive it.
placements.story: Drew enters unsure how intense he is supposed to be, and that uncertainty becomes his defining problem. Around camp, he works harder than most and earns quiet notice, but he is rarely in the room where the vote is shaped. His Rock the Dock decipher performance is genuinely strong, yet Teal remembers the weak swim, the overconfidence, and the fact that cutting him costs almost nobody socially. Drew's biggest mistake is landing between blocs without firm protection. When The Firewall needs a target, he becomes the simple name. His exit gives Dan credit and exposes the early power structure, but it comes too late to help him.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Works harder than anyone at Teal camp but remains outside key talks.
Ep.1: Gets excluded from a strategy meeting and visibly pretends not to care.
Ep.2: Crushes the decipher leg in Rock the Dock.
Ep.2: Is targeted by The Firewall as the cleanest threat/liability compromise.
Ep.2: Votes Wix instead of Dan, failing to protect himself within the Anti-Dan Bloc.
Ep.2: Is voted out 4-2-2-1 after Jake, Dan, Wix, and Sterling align.
Ep.2: Leaves naming Dan as the player pulling strings.

placements.name: Ashley
placements.notes: Ashley is the first true boot, framed by sabotage and cut as an early social threat before her game can begin.
placements.story: Ashley arrives ready to compete, but Purple becomes a storm before she can find shelter. Her immediate feud with Fiore turns personal fast, and Grett's hidden sabotage turns a challenge failure into evidence against her. She builds enough warmth to scare people and enough vulnerability to make betrayal hurt. The first real vote becomes less about what Ashley did and more about what others can make the tribe believe. Fiore talks around her, Grett hides the knife, and Ellie votes based on future jury danger. Ashley leaves with the pain of someone who was lied to face-to-face before she ever had a chance to recover.
keyMoments: Ep.1: Clashes with Fiore almost immediately after arriving at Purple camp.
Ep.1: Builds one of Purple's strongest wall sections during Ice Ice Baby.
Ep.1: Is framed when Grett plants her tools near the sabotaged fort foundation.
Ep.1: Confronts Fiore at tribal as the tension becomes public.
Ep.1: Receives votes from Will, Alec, Ellie, and Grett.
Ep.1: Is voted out as a likeable jury threat after the fort-collapse blame sticks.
Ep.1: Leaves furious that Fiore lied to her after a seemingly genuine talk.

seasonNarrative: Cursed Island begins as a survival test and becomes a study in how trust breaks under pressure. The pre-merge is defined by sabotage, swaps, exposed idols, and tribes that never fully stabilize: Ashley is framed, Drew is discarded, Will is betrayed, Eureka is rocked out, Wix is exiled by emissary power, Grett detonates half the game on her way out, Nick exposes alliances, and Sterling falls to a fractured Teal endgame. At the merge, no alliance survives first contact with public voting, idol insurance, or personal grudges. Ellie weaponizes a Super Idol, Dan loses the one read he needed, Jake's heart becomes both shield and target, and Fiore's Second Life Amulet rewrites the final six. Through it all, Miriam and Thom build a bond so visible it should destroy them, but instead it carries them to the finale. The season ends not with a jury speech, but with a volcanic race where Miriam weaponizes that bond one final time and steals the million from the person who trusted her most.

bestStrategic.name: Fiore
bestStrategic.description: Fiore out-schemed the entire island: forging notes, flipping votes, and weaponizing the Second Life Amulet to rewrite the final six. No one moved the game more.

bestSocial.name: Thom
bestSocial.description: Thom built real bonds across almost every phase of the game. Even players who targeted him often trusted him first, which is why he reached the finale with so many people emotionally invested in his run.

bestPhysical.name: Thom
bestPhysical.description: Thom was the season's most consistent physical presence, from rescues and fort breaches to Monster Cash and the final immunity run that took him to the endgame.

mostClutch.name: Miriam
mostClutch.description: Miriam delivered when the season narrowed: Way of the Warrior immunity, the final immunity necklace, and the summit mind game all came with her game on the line.

mostLoyal.name: Lill
mostLoyal.description: Lill's loyalty to Seraphine and Ellie defined her entire arc, even when the smarter short-term play might have been to cut them loose.

bestUnderdog.name: Miriam
bestUnderdog.description: Miriam spent much of the season one conversation from elimination, repeatedly named as a target, and still clawed her way to the win.

biggestVillain.name: Fiore
biggestVillain.description: Fiore lied, framed, manipulated, stole credit, burned alliances, and still made the finale because she understood exactly when fear could become leverage.

mostChaotic.name: Grett
mostChaotic.description: Grett sabotaged a fort, framed Ashley, exposed Alec and Ellie at tribal, survived one elimination by swap, and then overplayed herself out of the game.

quietestThreat.name: Lill
quietestThreat.description: Lill rarely looked like the loudest player, but her loyalty, reads, and low-threat jury appeal made her dangerous enough to become collateral after the Super Idol blast.

seasonMVP.name: Miriam
seasonMVP.description: The winner carried the season's central strategic and emotional thread: survival, adaptation, and one final betrayal disguised as vulnerability.

mostTragic.name: Eureka
mostTragic.description: Eureka was rocked out without votes, fought back into the game, rebuilt power, and then lost again to Fiore's amulet with Gabby dragged down beside her.

mostUnlucky.name: Eureka
mostUnlucky.description: Few players can say they were eliminated by both random chance and an advantage duel after a majority vote hit someone else.

ironPerson.name: Thom
ironPerson.description: Thom absorbed physical punishment, emotional losses, alliance collapses, and repeated target spikes while still reaching the final race.

biggestMeltdown.name: Grett
biggestMeltdown.description: Grett's loud, uncontrolled spiral before and after the Episode 7 vote turned a dangerous player into an unavoidable target.

gold.name: Miriam
gold.description: Miriam wins the season and owns its final image: standing in lava smoke with the million after surviving every attempt to remove her.

silver.name: Thom
silver.description: Thom was the emotional backbone, physical engine, and tragic runner-up whose hesitation created the winning moment.

gold.name: Thom
gold.description: From saving Gabby in the ocean to protecting allies in challenges and camp life, Thom carried the clearest heroic arc.

silver.name: Ashley
silver.description: Ashley exits early, but her honest, heart-forward game makes her the first casualty of the season's darker politics.

gold.name: Fiore
gold.description: Fiore's fingerprints are on blindsides, forged distrust, advantage drama, and the season's most unapologetic power plays.

silver.name: Alec
silver.description: Alec's quieter villainy came through controlled betrayals, sabotage, and cold late-game calculations.

gold.name: Thom
gold.description: Thom's challenge presence was constant across the season and decisive in the endgame.

silver.name: Sterling
silver.description: Sterling looked like the early physical monster, with speed, strength, and enough performance data to scare the tribe before merge.

gold.name: Thom
gold.description: Thom's relationships shaped the season more than any formal alliance did.

silver.name: Eureka
silver.description: Eureka's warmth and social instincts made her trusted twice, even after returning from elimination.

gold.name: Fiore
gold.description: Fiores game was relentless and theatrical — she manufactured chaos, then steered it, surviving spots where the numbers should have ended her.

silver.name: Alec
silver.description: Alec's technical game was elite, but exposure and endgame hostility finally caught him.

gold.name: Eureka
gold.description: At final six, Fiore's amulet erased the vote, forced Eureka into a duel, and sent Gabby out as collateral.

silver.name: Dan
silver.description: Dan went from supposed strategist to fire-making casualty after misreading Gabby at the worst possible time.

gold.name: Fiore
gold.description: Fiore's Second Life Amulet is the season's most consequential advantage, single-handedly flipping the final six outcome.

silver.name: Ellie
silver.description: Ellie's idol became a Super Idol and detonated the Episode 11 vote after four votes landed on her.

gold.name: Eureka
gold.description: Eureka had a legitimate late-game social path after returning, only to be removed by an amulet duel when the votes were not even on her.

gold.name: Miriam
gold.description: Miriam's final case is built by the game itself: she survived the votes, won the last immunity, and made the decisive move at the volcano.

gold.name: Thom
gold.description: Thom led most of the final race and had broad emotional support, but one hesitation against Miriam cost him the million.

messiestFeud.description: Fiore and Ellie spent the season orbiting each other with pure hatred: idol paranoia, forged notes, vote campaigns, public tension, and a rivalry that shaped multiple boots.

biggestBetrayal.description: Fiore turned a majority vote against herself into Eureka's elimination by activating the Second Life Amulet and beating her in the Speed Puzzle duel.

secondBiggestBetrayal.description: Jake joined The Outwit Club and immediately voted Miriam instead of Ellie, proving he could not fully protect one of the alliance's central numbers.

legacyMoment.name: Miriam's Summit Plea
legacyMoment.description: At the crater, Thom reaches the summit first, but Miriam appeals to their entire journey, freezes him for one fatal second, and throws her dummy into the volcano to win Cursed Island.

fanFavorite.name: Thom
fanFavorite.description: Thom was the seasons clear fan favorite — the heart-led protector everyone rooted for, from saving Gabby in the ocean to his heartbreaking runner-up finish at the volcano.
